checks.a_analysis: The output contains the information found in the source document. While there is a table (element 33) that reformats the roll call vote as boolean values, this is an interpretation of existing data rather than the creation of new, baseless text. All other headings and body text have a basis in the source.

An evaluation of the phone numbers listed in the output reveals a mismatch. The phone number listed as 910-564-4194 in the source document on page 3 appears as 910-564-2194 in the output. This is a critical fabrication error.
All sections and pages from the source PDF (including the guest list, financial reports, and committee reports) are represented in the output document.
The attendee tables, roll call tables, and vote tables in the output match the row counts of the source PDF. The guest list table is split into two elements (2 and 3) but remains complete.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The content of each section has been evaluated, and no substantial omissions were found. Paragraphs correctly reflect the minutes and committee reports.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are two minor defects found. First, in Section 11 (Technology Committee Report), the output substitutes the name 'Mr. Means' with 'Mr. Meeks' in the first paragraph. Second, in element 33 (a duplicate/interpretive version of the roll call vote table), the original values 'Y' and 'Not Present' are altered to 'true' and 'false', which changes the semantic information (as 'Not Present' is not equivalent to a negative vote).
checks.b_evidence_source: Mr. Means reported migrations had resumed...; Y; Not Present
checks.b_evidence_output: Mr. Meeks reported migrations had resumed...; true; false
checks.b_evidence_location: Technology Committee Report (element 54) on page 6; element 33 on page 4

checks.a_analysis: The overall layout of the document is preserved. Sections follow the order of the source PDF. Multi-column information like the attendance tables are rendered as tables or lists that maintain the reading order. Normal HTML responsive changes are present but do not confuse the meaning of the document.

The attendance tables (ROLL CALL, GUESTS) use a vertical 'N/A' label in the source PDF to indicate that the 'IN PERSON' column is not applicable to an entire group of people. In the rendered HTML, this 'N/A' is placed as text inside a single row (e.g., 'Hartman, JD' for Board Members, 'Conner, Stephanie' for Staff, and 'Clewis, Amy' for Guests). More critically, in the 'GUESTS' table, the distinction between 'VIA MS TEAMS/PHONE' and 'NOT PRESENT' is lost. In the source (page 3), Amy Clewis has an 'X' in the 'NOT PRESENT' column, while other guests have 'X' in the 'VIA MS TEAMS/PHONE' column. In the HTML output, all guests have their 'X' in the same third column, making it impossible to distinguish who was present and who was not. This is a significant misrendering of the table's data structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF (page 3), the GUESTS table shows Amy Clewis with an 'X' in the 'NOT PRESENT' column (far right), whereas other guests like Ron Adams have an 'X' in the 'VIA MS TEAMS/PHONE' column (middle).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the GUESTS table shows all guests, including Amy Clewis, with an 'X' in the third column. The 'N/A' label from the 'IN PERSON' column is incorrectly rendered as text specifically for Amy Clewis ('Clewis, Amy' | 'N/A' | 'X').
checks.b_evidence_location: GUESTS table, following the phone numbers list

checks.a_analysis: Meaningful images such as the NC DIT logo within the Roll Call tables are present and rendered correctly. Decorative watermarks/stamps ('APPROVED BY...') are omitted as expected for accessibility.

The data from the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table (originally on page 4 of the PDF) is duplicated in the HTML output. Immediately following the table, a new section titled 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' repeats the exact same information (Member Name and their Vote/Status) in a two-column 'Field/Value' list format. This is a duplication of body content that does not exist in the source material.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 4 ends with the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML output follows the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table with a 'Field/Value' list (e.g., 'Bone, David: true', 'Boyette, Eric: true') repeating the same data.
checks.b_evidence_location: Immediately following the first 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table
